title: poa pratensis subsp. nymanii
intro: Poa pratensis subsp. nymanii, also known as the meadow grass or Kentucky bluegrass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Poa pratensis, commonly found in temperate climates. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Poa pratensis subsp. nymanii can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues.
